definition: antiques are pieces of furniture and other items that are valuable because they are very old
example: an antique shop (a shop that sells antiques)
antique furniture

definition: a call is a sound or announcement that is intended to tell people that they need to do something
example: the call to prayer was heard all over the town
collocations: a call for someone to do something

example: I was called into the office on a Saturday

definition: communism is the political system that controlled Russia from 1917 and a lot of Eastern Europe from 1945 until 1991. They had a communist political system, and that period in their history is the communist period
example: the communist party of the Soviet Union
the collapse of communism in 1991
she became a communist during the war

definition: something that is decorated has been made to look beautiful by being painted or by having pretty things added to it
example: the church was beautifully decorated
we all helped to decorate the Christmas tree
why don’t we put up some party decorations?

definition: if you discover something, you find something that you did not know about before
example: they recently discovered some Roman ruins near here
scientists have made an exciting discovery

definition: a district is a particular part of a city or town
example: the main housing district of the city

definition: if you are a fan of something, you like it very much
example: I’m not a great fan of old buildings

definition: houses and apartment buildings where people live can be called housing
example: the industrial and housing district

definition: lively means full of life, energy, enthusiasm or people enjoying themselves
example: a very lively class

definition: something that is medieval was built or existed between 1000 AD and 1500 AD
example: a medieval church

definition: a mine is a very big, deep hole where people dig and bring up coal, gold, salt, etc, from under the ground
example: we went on a tour round a salt mine
a coal miner
a mining town
it’s too expensive to mine for coal here

definition: a mosque is a religious building where Muslims go to pray
example: there’s a beautiful mosque on the central square

definition: a palace is a large building, especially one where a king, queen, or president lives
example: an old royal palace by the river

definition: prayer is the act of speaking to God
example: we heard the call to prayer coming from the mosque
let us pray

definition: if you restore something, you repair it so that it is as good as it was when it was new
example: we must restore peace to the country after the war
restoration of the cathedral took over seven years

definition: in a theme park, the rides are the machines which move around and which you can go on for fun
example: he’s too young to go on the ride

definition: ruins are the parts of an old building that are still left after most of the building has been destroyed
example: there are some Roman ruins near the city
an old ruined temple

definition: a stall is a table outside, for example in a market, where people sell things. A person who sells things there is called a stallholder
example: a market stall

definition: you use underground to describe things that are under the surface of the earth
example: the underground world of the salt mines
coal was brought up from deep underground

definition: a wall is the outer part of a building or room, that goes round all the sides
example: there was a window set into the wall above the door

definition: accommodation is a place such as a house, flat, or hotel where you can live or stay for a while
example: how was the accommodation on your holiday?
a holiday cottage that can accommodate six people

definition: when wind or air blows, it moves. If someone or something is blown over or blown away, they are forced to move uncontrollably by strong wind
example: I was nearly blown over

definition: if the weather is boiling, it is very hot. You can say you are boiling or boiling hot when you are very hot
example: they said it’s going to be boiling hot all week

definition: if it is chilly, it is rather cold
example: it gets very chilly at night
there’s a slight chill in the air
there’s a slight chill in the air

definition: if something unpleasant clears or clears up, it gets better. If you clear something or clear it up, you make it better or tidier
example: they said the weather would clear up next week

definition: something that is constant happens all the time without stopping
example: the children needed constant attention
he was constantly changing his mind

definition: a cottage is a small house, especially one that is in a village or in the country
example: we rented a small cottage near the beach

definition: when an amount or level drops, it goes down. When the temperature drops, it becomes colder
example: it might drop to minus 10° tonight
a sudden drop in temperature

definition: entertainment is things that give people pleasure, like films, concerts,  television, etc
example: there’s plenty of entertainment in the evenings
it was a hugely entertaining film
the show is sure to entertain parents as well as children

definition: if you get away from somewhere, you go somewhere else, for example to avoid a situation that is difficult or uncomfortable
example: we try and get away for a few weeks during the summer

definition: something that is guaranteed will definitely happen
example: we can offer guaranteed good weather in July
we guarantee our cookers for fifteen years
we guarantee our cookers for fifteen years

definition: if the weather is humid, it is very hot and there is a lot of water in the air
example: it’s going to be hot and humid during the day
I can’t stand the humidity here

definition: if someone is miserable, they are very sad. If the weather is miserable, there is not much sunshine and it is cold or rainy
example: I felt a bit miserable when everyone had left

definition: if a place offers certain qualities or opportunities, they are available there
example: the countryside is very green and offers something different

definition: a season is the months of the year when a particular activity happens. For example, the holiday season is when most people take their holidays; the football season is the time of year when football is played
example: I take a holiday when the season ends

definition: a show is a performance in a theatre
example: we’re going to a show tonight

definition: when there is a storm, lots of rain falls and there are strong winds
example: there was a terrible storm last night
stormy weather

definition: if you take something down, you move it from where it is, often by removing pieces from it
example: we should take down the parasol or it will blow away

definition: if something is warm, it is slightly hot in a pleasant way
example: the weather was nice and warm
I’m cold – I need to warm up a bit
she lay in the warmth of the bed

definition: when it is windy, the wind is blowing very hard
example: it’s going to be really windy on the mountain
the wind is blowing from the east

definition: to commemorate people or events from the past means to show respect for them by having a special event or creating a special object
example: a ceremony to commemorate the anniversary of the island’s independence
the 70th anniversary commemoration event was held in the town hall
a commemorative stamp was issued by the post office

definition: to destroy something means to damage it so badly that it no longer exists
example: the city was completely destroyed by a volcano
we need to prevent the destruction of the environment

definition: if you fancy something or fancy doing something, you think you would like it or like to do it
example: do you fancy a coffee?

definition: your initial reaction or response to something is the first thing you say or do as a result of it
example: my initial reaction was to scream

definition: your reaction to something is what you do, say, or think as a result of it
example: my initial reaction was to scream
people react to fear in different ways

definition: if you take a risk, you do something which you hope will succeed, although you know it will be bad for you if it does not succeed
example: are you willing to take the risk?
I don’t want to risk my life
it’s risky to invest all your money in shares

definition: if you say that you are willing to do something, you mean that you will easily agree to do it
example: are you willing to take the risk?
I would willingly pay extra for a seat in the front row
his willingness to help was appreciated
he was unwilling to go to the police
rather unwillingly, she agreed to the proposal
